title: September 5th, 2021 (2)
body:
- lyrics
    - they said my brain couldn't be trusted
    - but they didn't know what trust was
    - they said my brain wouldn't...couldn't be trusted because
    - but then they only pointed at all the ways my brain wasn't perfect
    - i'm aware. i learned it while i was working
- i don't know when the last time i felt empathy from amber was. i don't know if she's able to see where i'm going and offer me info for that journey instead of continually pressuring me to carry things that work on a journey she has mapped out for me
- what did i do to deserve so little compassion
    - nothing
    - i expect people to treat me worst or be with me least when i'm struggling most
- work never gets done on accident. product is almost entirely accidental
    - so why do we think and act like we're young to get our work done on accident while making something on purpose
- while settling on the current organizational structure what alternatives have stakeholders considered. and how have different models been evaluated and compared in terms of company health, worker fulfillment, social responsibility, and mission
- the wheel of construction and deconstruction lined up with vedic wisdom terminology
    - the world grounds our perspective
        - dharma to maya
        - our perspective informs our experience
            - maya to [[[[[[[[art]]]]]]]]ha
            - our experience grounds our principles
                - [[[[[[[[art]]]]]]]]ha to sanskara
                - our principles inform our goals
                    - sanskara to kama
                    - our goals ground our strategies
                        - kama to karma
                        - our strategies inform our objectives
                            - karma to
                            - our objectives ground our tactics
                                - to yoga
                                - our tactics inform our affordances
                                    - yoga to
                                    - our affordances ground our choices
                                        - to action
                                        - our choices inform the world
                                            - moksha
    - when i ponder wandering a simulated universe
    - i thank god i'm not alone wasting cycles on this verse
    - when i ponder wandering simulated universes
    -
